description: Stochastic oscillator < 20 -> 2, else 0
fetch_method: Scanner filter FILTER_TYPE_STOCHASTIC_OSCILLATOR is broken (server bug). Use get_equity_historicals(symbols, start_time, interval=day) - pull ~14+ trailing daily bars and compute %K = (Close - Lowest Low(14)) / (Highest High(14) - Lowest Low(14)) * 100 client-side.
notes: Live-tested on NVDA 2026-07-07: real %K=23.77 computed from 44 daily bars on the first attempt - see reports/rubric-fetch-verification-2026-07-07.csv. Was 'not yet implemented' for weeks despite data being confirmed available; the gap was the calc code never being written, not fetchability. Implemented and verified now.

description: Price below lower Bollinger Band -> 3, else 0
fetch_method: Scanner filter FILTER_TYPE_BOLLINGER_BAND is broken (server bug). Use get_equity_historicals(symbols, start_time, interval=day) - pull ~20 trailing daily bars, compute the 20-day SMA and standard deviation of Close, lower band = SMA - 2*stdev, compare current price.
notes: Live-tested on NVDA 2026-07-07: SMA=202.33, upper=214.02, lower=190.64 computed from the same 44-bar historicals pull used for a4/a6/c18/c22/c23 - one fetch, six criteria. See reports/rubric-fetch-verification-2026-07-07.csv.

description: Price at/below computed Support level -> 3, else 0
fetch_method: Scanner filter FILTER_TYPE_SUPPORT is broken (server bug). Use get_equity_historicals(symbols, start_time, interval=day) - approximate support as the trailing 20-day low from the raw bars (documented, chosen heuristic - see rubric.md).
notes: Live-tested on NVDA 2026-07-07: trailing-20d low=189.80 vs close=195.55, computed cleanly on the first attempt. 'Support' is inherently fuzzier to define than Stochastic/Bollinger (no single universally-agreed formula) - the trailing-20d-low heuristic is now the documented standard, see rubric.md's 'Before scoring a criterion' section.

description: MACD histogram turned positive in last ~2 weeks -> 4
fetch_method: get_equity_historicals(symbols, ~40+ daily bars) -> compute EMA(12)/EMA(26), MACD line=EMA12-EMA26, signal=EMA(MACD,9), histogram=MACD-signal, client-side; scan for a sign-flip in the trailing ~10 sessions.
notes: Live-tested on NVDA 2026-07-07: full histogram series computed from 44 daily bars, last value -0.784, no positive crossover in the trailing 10 sessions -> correctly scores 0 (not N/A). IMPORTANT: the scanner's own FILTER_TYPE_MACD filter (interval=1d, length=14) also returns a value, but it does NOT match standard 12/26/9 MACD parameters (single length param, not three) and gave a materially different number (-2.66) on the same day - don't use the scanner snapshot for this criterion, use the historicals-based client-side calc, which is the only one that actually answers the 'turned positive' trend question.

Scanner filter FILTER_TYPE_RELATIVE_VOLUME confirmed still unreliable (returned exactly 1.0 for every candidate across 4 scans on 2026-07-07 earlier this session) - use this client-side calc instead, not the scanner filter.

NVDA's RSI never dipped below 30 in this window, so the calc correctly found no crossover event and scored 0 - not N/A. That's the calc working correctly (a real computed answer), not a fetch failure; the code is now implemented and reusable.
